steps.html: From discrete mathematics & data structures laying the groundwork...
...to algorithms, networks & databases building the toolkit...
...to robotics, software engineering & computer organization tying it all together;

intro.summary: I've compiled every lab, assignment & project from my journey through UCM's Computer Science & Engineering program!

items.NAME: 🤖 CSE 180
items.TITLE: Robotics
items.TEXT:
• Programmed autonomous robot behaviors using C++ and ROS (Robot Operating System) on Linux, integrating sensor input with actuator control in real-time.
  • Developed CMake and Makefile build systems to compile and deploy robotics workspaces across multiple hardware configurations.
  • Completed homework assignments covering kinematics, sensor fusion, and motion planning for mobile robot platforms.
  • Collaborated with a partner to design, implement, and debug robotic control pipelines from perception to action.

items.NAME: 📦 CSE 165
items.TITLE: OOP
items.TEXT:
• Completed six labs and a final project exploring core OOP concepts — classes, inheritance, polymorphism, templates, and operator overloading — in C and C++.
  • Built progressively complex programs emphasizing clean abstractions, encapsulation, and modular design patterns.
  • Developed a comprehensive final project demonstrating mastery of object-oriented design principles and software architecture.
  • Practiced disciplined version control and code organization across a multi-lab coursework repository.

items.NAME: 🌐 CSE 160
items.TITLE: Networks
items.TEXT:
• Implemented four networking projects covering transport protocols, routing, and distributed communication using nesC, Python, and C.
  • Built a TCP connector module handling reliable data transfer, flow control, and congestion management from scratch.
  • Completed homework assignments on network architecture, protocol design, and the OSI/TCP-IP model.
  • Gained hands-on experience with sensor network programming in nesC alongside traditional socket programming in Python and C.

items.NAME: 💻 CSE 120
items.TITLE: Software Engineering
items.TEXT:
• Collaborated with a team to build MotleyMoves, a web application to streamline race creation and participant tracking for a local nonprofit running club.
  • Developed a serverless C#/.NET backend on Azure Function Apps with an Azure SQL Database, handling 15+ API endpoints for race management, GPS tracking, and medical records.
  • Built a mobile-friendly front-end with Mapbox-powered interactive race course maps, Google Sign-In authentication, and FullCalendar event views.
  • Practiced agile software engineering methodologies including requirements gathering, sprint planning, and iterative delivery.

items.NAME: 🗃️ CSE 111
items.TITLE: Databases
items.TEXT:
• Designed and implemented relational database schemas across eight labs and a final project using T-SQL and SQLite.
  • Wrote complex SQL queries involving joins, subqueries, aggregations, views, stored procedures, and triggers to solve real-world data modeling problems.
  • Built a C# database application integrating programmatic data access with SQL backend logic.
  • Studied normalization theory, ER modeling, transaction management, and indexing strategies for database performance optimization.

items.NAME: 📈 CSE 100
items.TITLE: Algorithms
items.MOTTO: The art of solving problems efficiently
items.TEXT:
• Implemented 17 algorithm labs in C++ covering sorting (insertion, merge, quick, heap, bucket, radix), searching (linear, binary), and advanced data structures (hash maps, BSTs).
  • Built graph algorithms including BFS/DFS traversal and minimum spanning trees (Kruskal's and Prim's).
  • Implemented dynamic programming solutions for rod cutting, matrix chain multiplication, and Huffman coding.
  • Analyzed time and space complexity of each algorithm, comparing theoretical bounds with empirical benchmarks via shell scripts.

items.NAME: ⚙️ CSE 31
items.TITLE: Computer Organization
items.MOTTO: Understanding the logic beneath the abstraction
items.TEXT:
• Programmed in MIPS assembly and C across 10 labs, 8 homework assignments, and 2 projects covering CPU architecture, memory hierarchy, and instruction-level parallelism.
  • Implemented low-level routines including arithmetic operations, string manipulation, stack management, and recursive procedures in MIPS assembly.
  • Built projects exploring processor datapath design and cache simulation, bridging the gap between hardware and software.
  • Studied instruction set architecture, pipelining, virtual memory, and I/O systems to understand how high-level code maps to machine execution.

items.NAME: 📚 CSE 30
items.TITLE: Data Structures
items.MOTTO: Building blocks used in every program & script
items.TEXT:
• Implemented fundamental data structures in C++ across 13 labs and 2 homework assignments — linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, heaps, and hash tables.
  • Built and tested each structure from scratch, emphasizing pointer manipulation, dynamic memory allocation, and algorithmic efficiency.
  • Studied the trade-offs between sequential and linked representations, balancing time complexity with memory overhead.
  • Developed debugging and testing practices for complex pointer-based data structures in a systems programming environment.

items.NAME: 🔢 CSE 15
items.TITLE: Discrete Mathematics
items.TEXT:
• Completed 11 written homework assignments and 4 Python programming assignments covering logic, proofs, set theory, combinatorics, and graph theory.
  • Implemented discrete math concepts programmatically in Python — truth tables, set operations, counting algorithms, and graph traversals.
  • Studied propositional and predicate logic, proof techniques (direct, contradiction, induction), relations, functions, and recurrence relations.
  • Built foundational mathematical reasoning skills essential for algorithm analysis, cryptography, and formal verification.

items.NAME: 🕹️ ROP Game Design
items.TITLE: Game Design
items.MOTTO: Designing interactive worlds from concept to controller
items.TEXT:
• Designed and developed 2D and 3D games in Unity using C#, iterating through full game development cycles from concept and prototyping to playtesting and polish.
  • Created game design documents covering mechanics, level design, player progression, and user experience for original game concepts.
  • Implemented core gameplay systems including player controllers, enemy AI, collision detection, scoring, and UI menus.
  • Collaborated with peers on team-based projects, managing asset pipelines, version control, and integration of art, audio, and code.

items.NAME: 🏗️ ROP Architecture
items.TITLE: Architecture
items.MOTTO: Designing structures from blueprint to build
items.TEXT:
• Created architectural drawings and floor plans using AutoCAD and Revit, applying drafting standards and dimensioning conventions.
  • Designed residential and commercial building models with attention to structural integrity, spatial flow, and building code compliance.
  • Built physical scale models and 3D-printed prototypes to visualize and present architectural concepts.
  • Studied architectural history, materials science, and sustainable design principles alongside hands-on CAD/CAM workflows.

items.NAME: ♨️ AP Java
items.TITLE: AP Computer Science A
items.TEXT:
• Studied object-oriented programming in Java covering classes, inheritance, polymorphism, interfaces, and abstract classes.
  • Implemented data structures and algorithms including arrays, ArrayLists, sorting (selection, insertion, merge), searching (linear, binary), and recursion.
  • Completed AP-style free-response and multiple-choice practice covering control flow, string manipulation, 2D arrays, and object design.
  • Scored on the AP Computer Science A exam, demonstrating proficiency in Java fundamentals and computational thinking.
